methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $135M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
SpliceBio is a Series B-stage biotech company that has raised $135M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 8 private peers in the Biotech & Health sector at the same stage, supplemented by 3 public-market reference points (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $135M raised proxy, implying a valuation of $256M. Peer-Median analysis benchmarks against five same-sector, same-stage companies: Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M). The median across these peers is $810M, with a range of $600M–$3.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because SpliceBio's revenue is not disclosed; the Biotech & Health sector P/S median of 7.8x across public peers is therefore not actionable here.

The two applicable methods produce a wide dispersion. The triangulated analysis suggests a range of $218M (low) to $931M (high), with a median of $533M. The substantial gap between stage-progression ($256M) and peer-median ($810M) reflects material uncertainty around which comparables most closely match SpliceBio's risk profile, technology maturity, and market opportunity. Critical unknowns include clinical-stage progress, IP strength, and whether the peer set's valuation multiples reflect sustainable biotechnology economics or transient market conditions.
